title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: The gardener is cutting the grass for us this afternoon.
‘I'm sorry I didn't help you with your homework,’ said Sam.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs with negative gerunds.
questions.secondSentence: Sam apologised (1) _________ me with my homework.

questions.firstSentence: I really regret not buying that house when I had the chance.
questions.explanation: Tests 'wish' + past perfect for past regret.
questions.secondSentence: I (1) _________ that house when I had the chance.

questions.firstSentence: It is possible that the letter got lost in the mail.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for possibility (may have + past participle).
questions.secondSentence: The letter (1) _________ lost in the mail.

questions.firstSentence: I didn't realize my mistake until the following day.
questions.secondSentence: Not until the following day (1) _________ my mistake.

questions.firstSentence: This car is much more expensive than the one I saw yesterday.
questions.explanation: Tests comparison using 'not nearly as... as'.
questions.secondSentence: The car I saw yesterday was (1) _________ this one.
